label: Equivariance, invariance & geometric priors

node.label: Equivariance, invariance & geometric priors
node.definition: Architectures preserve or ignore known transformations such as translation, rotation, permutation, or gauge symmetry.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Equivariance, invariance & geometric priors is to connect Geometry, topology & symmetry, Graph theory to downstream capabilities including Graph neural networks, Neural operators, FNOs & PINNs, Data augmentation & synthetic-data generation.
examples: substrate for Neural operators, FNOs & PINNs
substrate for Data augmentation & synthetic-data generation
limitations: Its benefit depends on implementation, scale, data, and hardware; the same mechanism can add complexity without improving the target workload. Its direct dependencies include Geometry, topology & symmetry, Graph theory.

dependencies.rationale: Equivariance, invariance & geometric priors uses Geometry, topology & symmetry as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Equivariance, invariance & geometric priors uses Graph theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Equivariance, invariance & geometric priors uses Linear algebra as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
